extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE sprinted from SAGAN's wheel inside final kilometer after MACHADO caught at 6km, using technical uphill corners.
tactical_patterns: MACHADO granted 22-minute gap reveals GC teams willing to allow deep time deficits when breakaway non-threatening overall.
VALVERDE positioned behind SAGAN's leadout through final technical corner, exploiting world champion as unwitting pilot through dangerous turn.
GARCÍA CORTINA opened sprint too early on uphill finish, allowing patient wheels behind to benefit from his wind-breaking.
Groupama-FDJ sat out chase work despite MOLARD in red, forcing sprinter teams to control while protecting GC position.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: demonstrated uphill sprint superiority and tactical patience on technical finish compared to pure sprinters
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: mechanical issues disrupted positioning preparation, then unable to hold off VALVERDE despite leading final corner
rider_signals.MACHADO Tiago: positive: survived solo longest from three-man break, showing endurance capacity under extreme heat conditions
rider_signals.GARCÍA CORTINA Iván: negative: poor sprint timing on uphill finish, accelerating too early and fading as launchpad for others
rider_signals.MOLARD Rudy: neutral: defended red jersey passively, using breakaway time gap to avoid team chase responsibility
